LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO APRIL-30-2025 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JOSH ANDERSON'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING ANSWERS FORM INTERROGATORIES; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF JOSH ANDERSON MOVES TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO HIS FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE, AND FOR MONETARY AND EVIDENTIARY SANCTIONS. FOR THE REASONS STATED HEREIN, THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION IN PART AND DENIES IT IN PART. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING, WITH A COVER LETTER DEMONSTRATING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. =(301/CVA)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT AMY BEASOM NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO MOTION TO EXPUNGE LIS PENDENS AND FOR STATUTORY LEGAL COSTS IS GRANTED. THE COMPLAINT ON FILE UPON WHICH A LIS PENDENS WAS RECORDED AND MAINTAINED FOR MONTHS, DOES NOT STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION AFFECTING TITLE OR POSSESSION. IT IS A COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES ONLY. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR COSTS AND FEES IS DENIED. IT IS NOT SUPPORTED BY ANY EVIDENCE REGARDING HOURS, RATES, WORK PERFORMED OR COSTS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Motion For Order Compelling Answers Special Interrogatories
Off Calendar Apr-30-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF JOSH ANDERSON's Motion For Order Compelling Answers Special Interrogatories.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Josh Anderson moves to compel further responses to his special interrogatories, set one, and for monetary and evidentiary sanctions. For the reasons stated herein, the court grants the motion in part and denies it in part.

The relevant factual history and procedural history are set out in the court's companion order concerning the motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories.

The court orders Beasom to provide further responses to the following special interrogatories: 20, 21, 23, 27, and 29. The court otherwise finds Beasom's supplemental responses in substantial compliance with her discovery obligations. Anderson largely objects to Beasom's other special interrogatory responses (including her supplemental responses) because he does not credit her representation that she no longer has documents or seeks further detail in her answers. A party's response that it lacks information is full and complete, even if the opposing party does not find the answer credible, and does not provide a basis to order further responses.

Anderson seeks sanctions. The court orders a reduced amount in light of Anderson's partial success; Beasom shall pay $250 to Anderson by May 15, 2025. Beasom's position here was largely substantially justified but Beasom has not yet responded to some of the interrogatories identified in Anderson's moving papers and on that basis is sanctioned. (See CCP 2030.290(c).) The court denies Anderson's request for evidentiary sanctions.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, with a cover letter demonstrating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. =(301/CVA)

3 - PLAINTIFF JOSH ANDERSON MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS (TENTATIVE RULING PART 2 OF 2)
Off Calendar Apr-30-2025 Off Calendar

Motion For Order Compelling Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Apr-30-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF JOSH ANDERSON's Motion For Order Compelling Production Of Documents.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Josh Anderson moves to compel further responses to his requests for production of documents, set one, and for monetary and evidentiary sanctions. For the reasons stated herein, the court grants the motion in part and denies it in part.

The relevant factual history and procedural history are set out in the court's companion order concerning the motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories.

Beasom has provided objection-free verified supplemental responses. Her responses aver that she has only two documents related to her mortgage in her possession, custody, or control. Beasom's obligation to produce documents extends to documents she can readily obtain from her agents such as a mortgage broker. It is not clear whether Beasom has made a diligent inquiry. In addition, CCP 2031.230 provides that "A representation of inability to comply with the particular demand for inspection ... shall affirm that a diligent search and a reasonable inquiry has been made in an effort to comply with that demand. This statement shall also specify whether the inability to comply is because the particular item or category has never existed, has been destroyed, has been lost, misplaced, or stolen, or has never been, or is no longer, in the possession, custody, or control of the responding party. The statement shall set forth the name and address of any natural person or organization known or believed by that party to have possession, custody, or control of that item or category of item." Here, where Anderson's RFPs relate to categories of documents that likely exist somewhere, such as documents about Beasom's bills, mortgage, and refinancing, even if they are no longer in Beasom's possession, Beasom is obliged to respond with the information required by 2031.230. Beasom is ordered to make further responses by May 15, 2025 to all RFPs in set one and to comply with 2031.230 in her amended responses.

Anderson seeks sanctions. The court does not award evidentiary sanctions in light of Beasom's supplemental response but does find Beasom's position lacks substantial justification. Beasom shall pay $500 in sanctions to Anderson by May 15, 2025.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, with a cover letter demonstrating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. =(301/CVA)

Motion For Order Compelling Answers Form Interrogatories
Off Calendar Apr-30-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF JOSH ANDERSON's Motion For Order Compelling Answers Form Interrogatories.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Josh Anderson moves to compel further responses to his form interrogatories, set one, and for monetary and evidentiary sanctions. For the reasons stated herein, the court grants the motion in part and denies it in part.

Anderson obtained an order of this court on January 17, 2025, compelling defendant Amy Beasom to respond, without objections, to the form interrogatories (along with special interrogatories, set one, and requests for production of documents, set one) by February 18, 2025. Beasom served verified responses dated February 17, 2025. (Sanders Dec. Ex. 4.) Anderson's meet and confer letter of February 28 pointed out deficiencies in the responses and requested supplementation by March 14, 2025. (Sanders Dec. Ex. 5.) On March 14, 2025, Beasom sent updated responses through counsel to Anderson's counsel's email address. (Young Dec. para. 3 & unnumbered exhibit.) Anderson apparently did not receive them and contends they are inadequate in any event.

The court orders Beasom to provide further responses to the following form interrogatories: 2.7; 15.1(c). The court otherwise finds supplemental Beasom's responses in substantial compliance with her discovery obligation. Beasom shall amend, with verifications, by May 15, 2025.

Anderson seeks sanctions. The court finds Beasom's opposition to the motion to be substantially justified and does not award monetary or evidentiary sanctions.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, with a cover letter demonstrating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. =(301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens And For Statutory Legal Costs Memorandum Of Points And Authorities Request For Judicial Notice; Declaration Of Jonathan Madison, Esq.
Off Calendar Nov-01-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Jonathan Madison; The Madison Law Firm; 415-779-3177; attorney for Defendant Amy Beasom.

Steven C. Sanders; Sanders & Associates; 916-376-8738; attorney for Plaintiff Josh Anderson.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Mr. Sanders contests the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT AMY BEASOM NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO MOTION TO EXPUNGE LIS PENDENS AND FOR STATUTORY LEGAL COSTS is GRANTED. The Complaint on file upon which a Lis Pendens was recorded and maintained for months, does not state a cause of action affecting title or possession. It is a complaint for damages only. Defendant's request for costs and fees is denied. It is not supported by any evidence regarding hours, rates, work performed or costs.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
